entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A crop of Asian and African origin investigated for genetic diversity and fertility restoration ability

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Geographical origin of some pearl millet populations

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Geographical origin of some pearl millet populations

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Physical characteristics of pearl millet populations including grain yield

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A specific morphological trait used for multivariate analysis and clustering

entities.name: FERTILITY RESTORATION ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of populations to restore fertility in cytoplasmic male sterility systems

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic system (CMS) used to assess fertility restoration ability

entities.name: SEASON
entities.description: Contrasting environmental periods during which populations were assessed

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: Significant diversity found among yield linked traits of the populations

entities.name: MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS
entities.description: Statistical method used to group populations based on grain yield and component traits

entities.name: CLUSTERING
entities.description: The process of grouping populations into five clusters based on genetic and morphological traits

entities.name: EARLY FLOWERING
entities.description: A trait linked with Asian origin populations

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A trait linked with Asian origin populations, specifically short height

entities.name: NUMBER OF TILLERS
entities.description: A trait linked with Asian origin populations, specifically more tillers

entities.name: PANICLE SIZE
entities.description: A trait linked with Asian origin populations, specifically small and thin panicles

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: A trait linked with Asian origin populations, specifically small seeded

entities.name: A1 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic male sterility system with the highest fertility restoration frequency

entities.name: A4 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic male sterility system used for identifying maintainer and restorer lines

entities.name: A5 CMS SYSTEM
entities.description: A specific cytoplasmic male sterility system used for identifying maintainer and restorer lines

entities.name: MAINTAINER LINES
entities.description: Lines developed from populations to maintain the CMS system

entities.name: RESTORER LINES
entities.description: Lines developed from populations to restore fertility in CMS systems

relationships.description: linked with small and thin panicles
